Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Inspection Of Defendants Dog And Request For Sanctions; Declaration Of Robert H. Marcereau In Support Of Motion
Off Calendar Sep-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. Robert Marcereau, Esq. of Marcereau Law Group (tel: 949-323-4890) for the Plaintiff.
Atty. David Lerch, Esq. of Cesari, Werner and Moriarty (tel: 650-991-5126) for Defendants Joseph Birch and Emma Anderson-Smith.
Atty. Pamela Bumatay, Esq. of Wilson, Elser, Moskovitz, Edelman & Dicker LLP (tel: 415-433-0990) for Defendants Waterton Residential, L.L.C. and WRPV XIV DH SF, L.L.C.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF ABRAHAM WOOD's Motion To Compel Inspection Of Defendants Dog And Request For Sanctions.

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Abraham Wood's motion to compel inspection of Defendants Joseph Birch and Emma Anderson-Smith's dog Monte is granted.

"A party may demand that any other party... permit the party... to inspect and to photograph, test, or sample any tangible things that are in the party's possession, custody, or control." (Code Civ. Proc., section 2031.010, subd. (c)). A party may obtain discovery regarding "any matter, not privileged, that is relevant to the subject matter." (Code Civ. Proc., section 2017.010.) Birch and Anderson-Smith are ordered produce Monte for inspection, since the proposed inspection is not overly broad and is relevant to Wood's punitive damages claim and Defendants' comparative negligence defense. Defendants' opposition focuses on privacy concerns if the inspection were to happen at Defendants' home; however, Wood's Demand for Inspection specifically says the inspection could happen at a "mutually agreed-upon place." (Pl.'s Demand for Inspection, p. 2, ll. 25-26.)

The parties are ordered to meet and confer in good faith within two weeks to select a mutually agreeable location and a date. Any party may make a renewed motion if the parties are unable to agree on those terms.

Code of Civil Procedure, section 2031.300, subdivision (c), provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction... against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to a demand for inspection... unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." Here, Defendants did not act with substantial justification in opposing the motion to compel, since they primarily raised privacy concerns that were irrelevant given the language of the Demand for Inspection. However, the motion is straightforward enough that the court does not award full fees to Wood. Defendants shall pay $3000 to Wood within 30 days of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross-Complaint Against Joseph Birch And Emma Anderson-Smith; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Pamela B. Bumatay
Off Calendar May-07-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
PAMELA B. BUMATAY, Esq., WILSON, ELSER, MOSKOWITZ, EDELMAN & DICKER LLP (415) 433-0990
Pamela.Bumatay@WilsonElser.com Attorney for Defendants WATERTON RESIDENTIAL, L.L.C.
(erroneously sued as WATERTON), and WRPV XIV DH SF, L.L.C.
DAVID LERCH, ESQ, ESQ., CESARI, WERNER AND MORIARTY (650) 991-5126 dmoriarty@cwmlaw.com
Attorney for Defendants Joseph Birch and Emina Anderson-Smith

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WATERTON RESIDENTIAL, L.L.C. (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS WATERTON), WRPV XIV DH SF, L.L.C.'s MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross-Complaint Against Joseph Birch And Emma Anderson-Smith.

Waterton Residential, LLC and WRPV XIV DH SF, LLC's motion for leave to file a cross-complaint against Joseph Birch and Emma Anderson-Smith is granted.

The parties cite inapposite authorities relating to the filing of a compulsory cross-complaint. (See, e.g., CCP 426.50; Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94.) The proposed cross-complaint for indemnity is a permissive cross-complaint subject to the requirements of CCP 428.10. "There are no compulsory cross-complaints against parties other than plaintiff." (The Rutter Group California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial 6:524 (2025).)

The proposed cross-complaint arises out of the same transaction or controversy and therefore the filing is proper under CCP 428.10(b). While Waterton could have filed this motion earlier, Birch and Anderson-Smith are not prejudiced since they have been parties to this case since its inception and the trial is set for November 17, 2025. The court further notes that denying this motion would serve little purpose. Waterton would be able to commence an action for indemnity against Birch and Anderson-Smith if it were ultimately found liable to plaintiff. The dispute should be resolved in one action.

Order signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
